title: Workflow — SLO error budget exhaustion response
code_snippet:
# Situation: order-api SLO budget critically low
# SLO: 99.9% availability over 30-day window
# Error budget: 43.2 minutes total (0.1% of 30 days)
# Current state:
#   - Burn-rate alert fired at 6x for past 8 hours
#   - Consumed 66% of monthly error budget in one incident
#   - Remaining budget: 14.7 minutes for rest of month
#   - 22 days remaining in SLO window
#   - Root cause patched, service recovered
# Question: what is the response workflow now?
expected_feedback: Workflow: error budget exhaustion response for order-api. (1) Immediate: confirm incident resolved, verify burn-rate alert cleared, update incident timeline. (2) Budget assessment: 14.7 min remaining over 22 days — any further incident of >15 min exhausts the budget entirely. Risk: HIGH. (3) Protective actions: freeze non-critical deploys to order-api until budget recovers, require canary deploys for any changes, tighten rollback SOP to <2 min detection. (4) Escalation: if budget reaches 0, trigger SLO breach incident — notify engineering lead, schedule post-mortem, evaluate whether to renegotiate SLO target with stakeholders. (5) Monitoring: add Grafana panel showing remaining budget percentage, set warning alert at 20% remaining and critical at 5% remaining. (6) Recovery: budget replenishes as the 30-day rolling window advances — earliest full recovery in 8 days when the incident falls off the window.
